data_description.device_profile: Select the appropriate device profile for your Zendure system. The profile contains tuned control parameters.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent. This is the central basis for all decisions.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional sensor from the Zendure integration that reports active BMS limits (e.g. charge or discharge lock).
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. This is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the AC battery power. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh (e.g. from the Zendure integration). If set, it overrides manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value will be used for relative summer/winter thresholds and additional PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts. Used to detect surplus.
data_description.price_now_entity: Sensor with the current electricity price (e.g. Tibber). Used for economic decisions.
data_description.price_export_entity: Sensor with price forecast data (e.g. Tibber / EPEX). Enables price pre-planning.
data_description.grid_mode: Choose how your grid consumption is measured: one combined sensor or separate sensors for import and export.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power for grid import and negative power for grid export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power (positive value only).
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power (positive value only).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Select entity from the Zendure integration that switches between charging (INPUT) and discharging (OUTPUT).
data_description.input_limit_entity: Number entity to set the charge power in watts.
data_description.output_limit_entity: Number entity to set the discharge power in watts.

reconfigure_grid.title: Configure grid measurement
reconfigure_grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power for import and negative power for export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

grid.title: Configure grid measurement
grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power for import and negative power for export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

data_description.device_profile: Select the appropriate device profile for your Zendure system. The control parameters are adjusted automatically.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional status sensor from the Zendure integration to detect active BMS locks.
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. This is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the AC battery power. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh (e.g. from the Zendure integration). If set, it overrides manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value will be used for relative summer/winter thresholds and additional PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts.
data_description.price_now_entity: Current electricity price. Enables price-based discharge.
data_description.price_export_entity: Price forecast data for the next hours. Enables intelligent charge planning.
data_description.grid_mode: Defines how grid import and export are measured.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A combined grid sensor (positive = import, negative = export).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Zendure AC mode (INPUT/OUTPUT). Controlled automatically by the integration.

init.title: Edit control profile
init.description: Select the section you want to edit. “General” contains shared profile parameters, “Charging” contains control values for charge power, and “Discharging” contains control values for discharge power.

general.title: Edit control profile – General
general.description: General control parameters of the active device profile.

data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and additional PV-related logic.
data_description.TARGET_IMPORT_W: Target value for a small intentional grid import. A higher value reduces the risk of unintended export but slightly increases grid consumption.
data_description.EXPORT_GUARD_W: Additional safety reserve against unintended export. Higher values make the control more cautious.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_DEFICIT_W: Minimum deficit at which discharge is kept active. Helps prevent fluttering with very small loads.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_OUTPUT_W: Minimum discharge power to keep discharge stably active. Prevents unnecessary switching on and off.
data_description.SOC_DISCHARGE_RESUME_MARGIN: Additional SoC distance above the configured minimum SoC that must be reached before discharge is re-enabled after an SoC lock. Prevents fluttering between charging and discharging when battery level is low, especially in the morning with fluctuating PV power.

charge.title: Edit control profile – Charging
charge.description: Control parameters for the charging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.CHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for charge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes charging smoother, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing charging power. Higher values increase charge power faster, but may lead to more unstable behavior.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when reducing charging power. Higher values reduce charge power faster, but may cause stronger jumps.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing charge power. Higher values make charging control faster, but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing charge power. Higher values slow charging down faster, but may behave less smoothly.

discharge.title: Edit control profile – Discharging
discharge.description: Control parameters for the discharging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.DISCHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for discharge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes discharge smoother, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing discharge power. Higher values increase discharge power faster, but may overshoot more easily.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when reducing discharge power. Higher values reduce discharge power faster, but may behave less smoothly.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing discharge power. Higher values make discharge control faster, but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing discharge power. Higher values slow discharge down faster, but may cause stronger jumps.

state.expensive_discharge: Discharging due to high electricity price
state.very_expensive_force_discharge: Forced discharge due to extreme price spike
state.soc_min_resume_block: Discharge blocked (re-enable threshold not yet reached)
